tweet.text:
Big news: Kimi-K3 by @Kimi_Moonshot is now #1 in the Frontend Code Arena with 1679 pts, surpassing Claude Fable 5.

This is a 17-place jump from Kimi-k2.6 (#18 -> #1).

In Frontend, Kimi-K3 ranked #1 in 6 of 7 domains: Brand & Marketing, Reference-Based Design, Data & Analytics, Consumer Product, Simulations, and Content Creation Tools, landing #2 only in Gaming behind Fable 5.

The full model weights will be released by July 27.

Congrats to the @Kimi_Moonshot team on this major milestone!

Uniswap governance extends protocol fee collection and UNI burn mechanism to Robinhood Chain –
Link
Uniswap submitted a governance proposal to extend protocol fee collection and the UNI burn mechanism to Robinhood Chain across v2, v3, and v4. Fees generated on Robinhood Chain would go into the chain's TokenJar contract, with UNI bridged back to Ethereum mainnet for burning.
Matters because:
Expanding fee capture to another chain could support Robinhood Chain's DEX ecosystem and increase UNI burn velocity.

tweet.text:
lots of conversations about base over the last week. wanted to share my candid take after a week of listening and a lot of reflection over the last 6 months.

first off - in case it’s not obvious, the first quarter of 2026 was a punch in the face. I spent 2024 and 2025 making a two pronged bet to bring base to the world: (1) builders would unlock the next wave of crypto adoption; (2) adoption would be driven by new onchain-native social experiences - creators, content, messaging. imo we made the right bet on builders, but obviously the wrong bet on social. builders did drive the next wave of crypto adoption - prediction markets, perpetuals, stablecoins - but social was not at the center of it. in fact, the entire social side of the market that many of us had been building towards - farcaster, zora, miniapps, and yes, creator coins - disintegrated completely. I was wrong - whether it was timing wrong (is $ansem a creator coin?) or fully wrong, only time will tell, but regardless, i was

tweet.text:
After spending the last two years building the MegaMafia, we’ve decided to sunset the program. 

While it was a success in many ways, we believe that the program was built on assumptions that no longer hold. 

Here are some reflections, and what’s coming up next.

> We had real conviction in Mafia founders, and have gone above and beyond in standing by their side to bring their products to market. Some examples include: restructuring the GTE leadership team during critical moments just one month after their initial pre-seed raise, offering to buy out Noise's investors when they tried to push out the CEO, and played a crucial role as they pivoted from their initial prediction market idea, and merging the Tenten and HitOne team after seeing the synergy between the two.

> Across both cohorts which in total included 20 teams, we lent our reputation and networks to founders so they could go on to raise over $80m in funding in pre-seeds, seeds, and series As. We kept the MegaETH’s raise to

tweet.text:
Big news: Kimi-K3 by @Kimi_Moonshot is now #1 in the Frontend Code Arena with 1679 pts, surpassing Claude Fable 5.

This is a 17-place jump from Kimi-k2.6 (#18 -> #1).

In Frontend, Kimi-K3 ranked #1 in 6 of 7 domains: Brand & Marketing, Reference-Based Design, Data & Analytics, Consumer Product, Simulations, and Content Creation Tools, landing #2 only in Gaming behind Fable 5.

The full model weights will be released by July 27.

Congrats to the @Kimi_Moonshot team on this major milestone!

Introducing Kimi K3: Open Frontier Intelligence

🔹 2.8 Trillion Parameters, 1 Million Context, Native Multimodal
🔹 Kimi Delta Attention enables up to 6.3x faster decoding in million-token contexts
🔹 Attention Residuals deliver ~25% higher training efficiency at <2% additional cost
🔹 Built for long-horizon agentic coding and self-evolving workflows

Kimi K3 is now live on on http://Kimi.com, Kimi Work, Kimi Code, and the Kimi API.
Open Weights by July 27, 2026.

🔗 API: http://platform.kimi.ai
🔗 Tech blog: http://kimi.com/blog/kimi-k3

Source:
https://www.kimi.com/blog/kimi-k3

Artemis x Visa: Agentic Payments from the Ground Up - A Guide to the Current State of Agentic Payments.
> Agentic commerce is gaining traction via x402 and MPP for autonomous micropayments.
> The protocols solve for speed, programmability, and cost. However, scaling requires trust infrastructure, compliance frameworks, merchant distribution, and dispute resolution that incumbent payment players have spent decades building.
> That gap defines the opportunity, and Visa has positioned itself across both models.
> This report examines the protocols, standards, data, and market structure forming around agentic payments today + the opportunities & challenges.
Source:
https://visa.com/api/image-proxy?path=%2Fcontent%2Fdam%2Fvisa%2Freimagine-visa%2Fthought-leadership%2Fdocuments%2Fagentic-payments-report.pdf

I spent 2024 and 2025 making a two pronged bet to bring base to the world: (1) builders would unlock the next wave of crypto adoption; (2) adoption would be driven by new onchain-native social experiences - creators, content, messaging. imo we made the right bet on builders, but obviously the wrong bet on social. builders did drive the next wave of crypto adoption - prediction markets, perpetuals, stablecoins - but social was not at the center of it. in fact, the entire social side of the market that many of us had been building towards - farcaster, zora, miniapps, and yes, creator coins - disintegrated completely. I was wrong - whether it was timing wrong (is $ansem a creator coin?) or fully wrong, only time will tell, but regardless, i was

tweet.text:
After spending the last two years building the MegaMafia, we’ve decided to sunset the program. 

While it was a success in many ways, we believe that the program was built on assumptions that no longer hold. 

Here are some reflections, and what’s coming up next.

> We had real conviction in Mafia founders, and have gone above and beyond in standing by their side to bring their products to market. Some examples include: restructuring the GTE leadership team during critical moments just one month after their initial pre-seed raise, offering to buy out Noise's investors when they tried to push out the CEO, and played a crucial role as they pivoted from their initial prediction market idea, and merging the Tenten and HitOne team after seeing the synergy between the two.

Japan reclassifies crypto assets as financial products, cutting tax from 55% to 20% –
Link
Japan's Senate passed amendments to the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act on July 15, reclassifying cryptocurrencies from payment instruments to financial products. The law cuts the top tax rate on crypto gains from 55% to 20% starting in January 2028, adds insider trading rules, and opens the path for spot Bitcoin ETFs around 2027.
Matters because:
Clearer rules and lower tax friction could make Japan a more attractive market for institutional crypto participation.

the biggest coins currently present on robinhood, the staples that made the chain what it has become today, even if still in its infancy

people loved the cat, it has been liberated

the legacy of the degens will stay forever

there is only one solution to avoid dilution of the tokens, the staples

The Residency Opens Applications for Physical AI & Robotics Founders Cohort  > The Residency is seeking and backing founders building durable physical AI and robotics startups focused on solving deep technical problems  To apply
👉
apply.livetheresidency.com/apply…

The Beginner’s Guide to Physical AI Stack
Physical AI encompasses industrial robots, warehouse automation, and autonomous vehicles
The 5 layer stack: Brain (AI/VLA models), Body (humanoid), Parts (actuators, gearboxes, sensors), Fuel (training data via teleoperation, simulation, and fleet experience), and Proving Grounds (actual deployments)
https://x.com/muskan_kalra24/status/2077090406819647871?s=20

Nobody is close to breaking ECC
Although theoretical advances have dramatically reduced the quantum hardware needed to break Elliptic Curve Cryptography, current quantum computers are still orders of magnitude away from having enough error-corrected qubits to execute the attack
Consequently, standard internet protocols and cryptocurrencies face no immediate risk, though exposed public addresses will eventually need to transition to quantum-resistant encryption
https://derrickcui.substack.com/p/nobody-is-close-to-breaking-ecc

Ostium perpetuals DEX suffers $18 million oracle exploit –
Link
Ostium, an Arbitrum-based perpetuals exchange, lost about $18 million on July 15 after hackers compromised an oracle signer key and submitted falsified future-dated price data to create fake trading profits.
Matters because:
The exploit reinforces how oracle security remains a key weak point for leveraged DeFi protocols.

The New Private Asset
Privacy tokens were delisted from exchanges 73 times in 2025, yet they became crypto's best-performing sector
Their underlying zero-knowledge cryptography restores fungibility and financial privacy - features structurally missing from Bitcoin
https://chamath.substack.com/p/privacy-tokens

Researchers successfully ran a multi-turn reinforcement learning post-training setup by using just 14 MacBooks across 4 countries to generate data and a single, centralized datacenter GPU to perform the training

Semiconductors/Compute.text:
U.S. inflation data sparks Bitcoin rally to $65,500 as Fed rate-hike expectations collapse –
Link
Bitcoin jumped to $65,500 on July 15 after June CPI came in at 3.5% year-over-year, below the 3.8% forecast, while core CPI eased to 2.6%. The move triggered about $300 million in short liquidations and pushed Fed rate-hike odds from 43% to 13%.
Matters because:
Cooling inflation removes a key macro headwind for BTC and risk assets, with the September FOMC now the next major checkpoint.

S. KOREA TO TEMPORARILY HALT NEW SINGLE STOCK LEVERAGED ETFS
SOUTH KOREA HIKES MINIMUM DEPOSIT REQUIREMENT FOR CHIP LEVERAGED ETF: INFINITYHEDGE
SOURCE

U.S. June CPI posts largest monthly decline since 2020, cooling Fed rate hike expectations –
Link
U.S. June CPI fell 0.4% month-over-month and 3.8% year-over-year, both below expectations. Core CPI was flat month-over-month at 2.6% year-over-year, versus 2.9% forecast. July Fed rate-hike odds fell from about 50% to 15%, though September odds remain 61%.
Matters because:
The softer inflation print lowers near-term rate-hike pressure and supports risk assets, even if oil-driven geopolitical risks keep later hikes in play.

How to Finally Make Something …or how we put off our creative dreams for our entire lives [old article]
Many avoid the anxiety of taking an unstructured path by playing "fantasy games" like
endless learning, collecting tools, obsessing over processes, or doing minor maintenance tasks
To defeat these distractions, you must adopt just-in-time learning, commit to your existing tools, and prioritize raw output over trying to do things the "right" way
